dataset: USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) - synthetic soybean processing case

pi_items.source: task.json description specifies 'Create a new process "Soybean Processing, at plant"'; export_result.sh queries for LOWER(NAME) LIKE '%soybean%'
task.json description item 2: 'Create two new product flows: Soybean Oil, crude (Mass) and Soybean Meal (Mass)'. Metadata uses simplified names without 'crude' qualifier.
task.json description item 4: 'configure Physical Allocation (Mass) so burdens are split based on output mass'. openLCA allocation manual confirms physical allocation uses mass as the basis.

task.json description item 3 specifies '(Oil: 0.185 kg, Meal: 0.795 kg)'. This represents 18.5% oil yield per kg of soybean input. Modern soybean crushing facilities achieve 18.5% oil extraction rates (CME Group). LCA studies use 17-19% range for oil extraction. This value is within the standard industrial range.

task.json description item 3 specifies '(Oil: 0.185 kg, Meal: 0.795 kg)'. This represents 79.5% meal yield per kg of soybean input. LCA studies (20.5% oil + 79.5% meal after excluding hulls/waste) use mass allocation of 79.5% meal. This value is consistent with peer-reviewed soybean LCA literature.
Calculated from oil_amount and meal_amount: 0.185/(0.185+0.795) = 18.87%, 0.795/(0.185+0.795) = 81.13%. Task description states '~18.9%, ~81.1%' which matches these calculated values.

setup_task.sh lines 22-27 check for USLCI_ZIP at /home/ga/LCA_Imports/uslci_database.zip and copy from /opt/openlca_data/uslci_database.zip if needed. USLCI is a real, publicly available database maintained by NREL and Federal LCA Commons since 2001.

TRACI 2.1 is a real LCIA method published by US EPA in 2012 (Bare 2012). It provides environmental impact assessment with characterization factors for 10+ impact categories including Global Warming Potential (GWP). LCIA methods are available for import into openLCA.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic soybean processing case in openLCA using standard industrial parameters. Oil yield of 18.5% (0.185 kg) and meal yield of 79.5% (0.795 kg) per kg input are realistic values consistent with modern soybean crushing (17-19% oil extraction) and peer-reviewed LCA literature. Physical allocation of environmental burdens is configured using mass (Oil ~18.9%, Meal ~81.1%), which is a standard methodology in soybean LCA studies. The USLCI database is a real, publicly available US Life Cycle Inventory database maintained by NREL. TRACI 2.1 is a real EPA-developed LCIA method suitable for calculating environmental impacts. The output CSV is exported to /home/ga/LCA_Results/soybean_allocation.csv.
